reviews.review: The sync feature is the entire reason I bought this and it has never once worked across my two devices. Two stars would be generous.
Crashes every time I have more than fifty notes. The one job it has, it cannot do at scale.
Lost a month of entries after an update with no warning and no backup. Core data integrity is broken.

voice: If the core feature doesn't work reliably, nothing else on the roadmap matters.
I don't care about the AI assistant if the save button loses my work.
Every update seems to break the one thing I actually depend on.
Reliability is a feature. It's the only feature, really, until it's solved.
Show me uptime, not vision. I've been burned by vision before.
The demo always works. My real workload is where these things fall apart.
I'll tolerate an ugly UI. I will not tolerate data loss.
Fix the foundation before you paint the walls.

reviews.review: Advertised a no-logs policy, then the privacy report quietly changed. I no longer trust a word they say.
Free plan suddenly required a card on file 'for verification.' That's not verification, that's a trap.
Buried the data-sharing toggle three menus deep and defaulted it on. Dark pattern, plain and simple.

voice: The moment a company changes its privacy policy quietly, I'm out.
Default-on data sharing tells me exactly how they see their users.
Trust is the only thing they can't ship in an update.
I read the terms now. I've been burned too many times not to.
If the opt-out is hidden three menus deep, that's a decision, not an accident.
A 'free' tier that wants my card up front isn't free, it's bait.
They'll get my benefit of the doubt exactly once.
Transparency is cheap to offer and they still won't offer it.

reviews.review: Opened a support ticket about a billing error nine days ago. Still nothing but an autoresponder. Unacceptable.
The help docs describe a UI that hasn't existed for two versions. Support told me to 'check the docs.'
Got charged twice, support closed my ticket as 'resolved' without resolving anything. Fighting my bank now.

voice: A product is only as good as what happens when it breaks, and here nothing happens.
Autoresponders are not support. They're a way to look busy while ignoring me.
Outdated docs plus absent humans equals a product you're on your own with.
I judge companies by their worst day of support, not their best demo.
Closing my ticket as 'resolved' to hit a metric is its own kind of insult.
When the help center is a maze, that's a choice to not be helped.
I've stopped expecting fast. I'd settle for 'a human read this.'
The silence after you pay is the loudest signal there is.

reviews.review: Used to be a clean writing tool. Now it's a bloated suite with five tabs I'll never open. Lost the plot.
I wanted to track runs. Now there's a social feed, a store, and badges. Just let me track a run.
Every update adds a feature and removes a setting. The thing I used it for is now buried under noise.

voice: Feature creep is how good products forget what they were for.
I don't want a platform. I wanted the one thing you started with.
Every new tab is a tax on the people who just wanted the original feature.
The roadmap is long because saying no is harder than shipping yes.
Adding is easy and addictive. Removing is the discipline nobody has.
Notifications for features I never asked for are a fast way to lose me.
The first version had a point of view. Now it has a backlog.
I'm not impressed by how much it does. I'm tired by how much it does.

reviews.review: After a week I still can't tell what this does that my existing tools don't. The pitch is vibes, not value.
Beautiful charts of data I already had in a place I already trusted. Solves nothing for me.
Paid for a year, never figured out the point. That's on me, but also on a product that never explained itself.

voice: If I can't explain what it does for me in one sentence, it doesn't do anything for me.
Pretty is not the same as useful, and this confuses the two.
The pitch was vibes. I needed value and got an aesthetic.
I keep waiting for the moment it earns its place and it never comes.
A solution is supposed to leave me with one less problem, not the same problems, prettier.
I gave it a real week. The verdict is a shrug.
Show me the before and after. If there's no after, there's no product.
It's not bad. It's worse than bad — it's pointless, expensively.
